content:
✨ WeClone | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To create a personalized AI digital avatar that mimics a user's communication style by fine-tuning an LLM on their chat history.
✨ Key Features: • End-to-end solution for creating digital avatars • Chat data export and preprocessing • LLM fine-tuning with chat history (including image data) • Integration with Telegram and other platforms (WhatsApp, Discord, Slack, WeChat)
📖 Summary: WeClone is a Python-based solution for creating a personalized AI digital avatar from chat history. It allows users to fine-tune Large Language Models (LLMs) with their chat logs, capturing their unique communication style, and then deploy this model as a chatbot, effectively bringing their digital self to life.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 oxwm | Zig
🎯 Primary Use Case: A highly configurable and user-friendly dynamic window manager for Linux/Unix-like systems, written in Zig.
✨ Key Features: • Dynamic window management in Zig • Lua API for configuration • Hot-reloading of configuration • LSP-powered autocomplete • Sane defaults
📖 Summary: OXWM is a dynamic window manager written in Zig, offering an enhanced alternative to dwm. It provides a flexible Lua API for configuration with hot-reloading, aiming to reduce user friction through sensible defaults, LSP integration, and instant configuration updates without restarting the X session.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 Fun-ASR | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Accurate and efficient speech-to-text transcription, especially in specialized domains and multilingual environments.
✨ Key Features: • End-to-end speech recognition large model • Trained on tens of millions of hours of real speech data • Powerful contextual understanding capabilities
📖 Summary: Fun-ASR is an advanced, end-to-end speech recognition large model developed by Tongyi Lab. It is trained on massive datasets, offering robust contextual understanding and industry-specific adaptability. The model supports low-latency real-time transcription across 31 languages and excels in specialized domains by accurately handling jargon and minimizing errors like hallucinations.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 Nano-PDF | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Editing PDF documents, particularly presentations or reports, by describing desired changes in natural language, making PDF modification more accessible and efficient.
✨ Key Features: • Natural Language Editing • Add New Slides • Non-Destructive Editing (OCR Re-hydration) • Multi-page & Parallel Processing
📖 Summary: Nano-PDF is a Python-based command-line tool that allows users to edit PDF documents using natural language prompts. It leverages Google's Gemini 3 Pro Image model to understand and execute edits, such as updating text or generating new content, while preserving the original document's structure and text layer through OCR.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 pgschema | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: Automating and simplifying PostgreSQL schema migrations using a declarative, Terraform-like approach, especially for complex schemas with advanced PostgreSQL features.
✨ Key Features: • Terraform-style declarative schema migration • Automatic DDL generation from desired state • Support for advanced PostgreSQL-specific objects (RLS, partitioning, triggers, etc.)
📖 Summary: pgschema is a Go-based CLI tool that enables Terraform-style declarative schema migrations for PostgreSQL. It allows users to define their desired database schema state and automatically generates the necessary SQL migration DDL, simplifying the migration process and supporting advanced PostgreSQL features.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
